data.title: Spot silver touched $72 per ounce, a new low since April 7.
data.description: According to Gate data, spot silver touched $72 per ounce, a new low since April 7, down 1.53% on the day. The US dollar index (DXY) rose to an intraday high of 98.77. Both WTI and Brent crude oil prices continued to rise in the short term, last quoted at $105.75 and $107.79 per barrel, respectively.

data.title: Standard Chartered: DeFi Resilient Despite KelpDAO Exploit Impact
data.description: Standard Chartered has expressed confidence in the resilience of decentralized finance (DeFi) despite recent setbacks. According to NS3.AI, the bank described DeFi as "bent, not broken" following the KelpDAO exploit on April 18, which resulted in $292 million in losses and affected the AAVE platform. The incident led to a 38% decline in AAVE deposits and a 31% drop in active loans, as stolen tokens were used as collateral for borrowing other assets. Geoff Kendrick from Standard Chartered remains optimistic, projecting that tokenized real-world assets will achieve a market capitalization of $2 trillion by the end of 2028, a significant increase from $35 billion in October 2025.

data.title: The market predicts that trading volume will exceed $25 billion in March, with retail investors driving growth and crypto becoming the main entry point.
data.description: Data shows that the total trading volume of the prediction market reached $25.7 billion in March 2026, a 10.6% increase month-over-month. Trading activity was highly concentrated among small-scale retail users. Of the 1.29 million wallets tracked in the first quarter, 82.3% of users traded less than $10,000. Growth primarily came from micro, light, and moderately active user groups, with the market exhibiting a structure of "high-frequency participation and low single-transaction size." Crypto assets were the main entry point to the prediction market, accounting for 39.6% of micro-user activity. Bitcoin-related event contracts attracted approximately 593,000 users in the first quarter, with a trading volume of $5.42 billion, making it the most participated crypto prediction market. Ethereum and Solana recorded trading volumes of $1.19 billion and $420 million, respectively. In terms of category structure, the sports market ranked first with a trading volume of $10.1 billion, followed by the political market ($5 billion), of which geopolitical related transactions accounted for $2.41 billion. The overall trading volume of the crypto-related market reached $7.3 billion. Analysis suggests that predicting market growth relies primarily on "category expansion" rather than an increase in the size of individual transactions. Micro-users are active for an average of 2.5 days and participate in 1.45 categories, while medium-sized users are more active, participating in 9.9 days and 2.34 categories, indicating a shift from single-market speculation to a structure of sustained multi-market participation. (The Block)

data.title: Prediction Market Transactions Reach $25.7 Billion in March 2026
data.description:
In March 2026, prediction markets saw a total transaction volume of $25.7 billion, marking a 10.6% increase from the previous period. According to Odaily, the trading activity was predominantly concentrated among small retail users. Of the 1.29 million wallets tracked in the first quarter, 82.3% of users engaged in transactions below $10,000. The growth was mainly driven by micro, light, and moderately active user groups, reflecting a market structure characterized by high-frequency participation with low transaction sizes.

Cryptocurrency assets served as the primary entry point for prediction markets, accounting for 39.6% of micro-user activity. Bitcoin-related event contracts attracted approximately 593,000 users in the first quarter, with a transaction volume of $5.42 billion, making it the most engaged crypto prediction market. Ethereum and Solana recorded transaction volumes of $1.19 billion and $420 million, respectively.

In terms of category structure, sports markets led with a transaction volume of $10.1 billion, followed by political markets at $5 billion, with geopolitical-related transactions accounting for $2.41 billion. Overall, crypto-related markets reached a total transaction volume of $7.3 billion.

Analysts suggest that the growth in prediction markets is primarily driven by 'category expansion' rather than an increase in single transaction sizes. Micro users were active for an average of 2.5 days, participating in 1.45 categories, while medium users increased their activity to 9.9 days, engaging in 2.34 categories, indicating a shift from single speculative activities to sustained participation across multiple markets.

data.title: Ether.fi CEO Addresses Contribution to Kelp Hack Recovery Fund
data.description: Ether.fi CEO Mike Silagadze has addressed the decision to contribute 5,000 ETH to the Kelp hack recovery fund. According to Odaily, Silagadze expressed concerns about the potential risk the incident poses to the entire DeFi ecosystem. He warned that if Kelp were to go bankrupt, $1.5 billion worth of rsETH could be frozen for an extended period, potentially halting the $30 billion Aave lending market and triggering a chain reaction affecting both DeFi and CeFi sectors. Silagadze compared the situation to the FTX collapse, suggesting it could be more significant. He emphasized the importance of taking responsibility and quickly raising funds to prevent the worst-case scenario, contrasting this proactive approach with other institutions that have opted to rely on legal solutions.

data.title: U.S. President Donald Trump Discusses Energy Production with Executives
data.description: U.S. President Donald Trump held a meeting with energy company executives on Tuesday to address various issues related to domestic production, Venezuela, oil futures, natural gas, and shipping. According to NS3.AI, White House officials revealed details of the meeting, highlighting the administration's focus on energy sector challenges and opportunities.

data.title: ECB Rate Decision Could Impact Euro, TD Securities Strategists Say
data.description: TD Securities strategists have indicated that the euro may decline if the European Central Bank (ECB) decides to keep interest rates unchanged on Thursday and fails to commit to a rate hike in June as anticipated by the market. According to Jin10, ECB President Christine Lagarde is expected to emphasize uncertainties and the risks of rising inflation and slowing growth. She may suggest that more data is needed to assess the medium-term impact of rising energy prices on inflation. The strategists noted that if questioned about the possibility of a rate hike in June, Lagarde might reiterate that the ECB is prepared to act, provided the data supports such a move.

data.title: Pakistan Awaits Iran's Response on U.S. Talks, Says Prime Minister
data.description: Pakistan's Prime Minister Shahbaz Sharif announced that Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi has promised to respond regarding the prospects of Iran-U.S. talks after consulting with Iranian leadership. According to Odaily, Shahbaz shared this information during a cabinet meeting, noting that Araghchi led a delegation to Pakistan last week for comprehensive discussions with Pakistani officials. Before departing, Araghchi assured Shahbaz that he would provide feedback following consultations with Iran's leadership. Shahbaz also mentioned that the ceasefire achieved between Iran and the U.S., facilitated by Pakistan, remains in effect.

data.title: The total investment in the White House Trump banquet hall has risen to $400 million, with several crypto companies previously disclosed to have participated in the donation.
data.description: Fortune magazine revealed that the total investment in the White House Trump banquet hall has increased from the initial estimate of $200 million to $400 million. The funds primarily come from tax-deductible private donations, which will be managed through the non-profit organization Trust for the National Mall. Fortune previously reported that several crypto companies and individuals participated in the donation, including Coinbase, Ripple, Tether America (a subsidiary of Tether), and prominent figures in the crypto industry such as Paxos co-founder Charles Cascarilla, Cameron Winklevoss, and Tyler Winklevoss. In addition, major technology companies such as Meta, Apple, Amazon, Google, and Microsoft are also among the donors. Notably, Google's parent company, Alphabet, previously reached a settlement with Trump and pledged $22 million for the banquet hall's construction.

data.title: A senior Iranian official stated that Iran will respond to US "piracy" with unprecedented military action.
data.description: A senior Iranian security official stated that Iran will soon respond to the United States' "continued piracy" with "unprecedented military action." (CCTV News)

data.title: US Senator Warren: Trump is pushing the Federal Reserve into a predicament
data.description: U.S. Democratic Senator Elizabeth Warren stated that Trump is pushing the Federal Reserve into a difficult situation. (Jinshi)

data.title: Senator Warren Criticizes Trump's Pressure on Federal Reserve
data.description: U.S. President Donald Trump is reportedly putting the Federal Reserve in a difficult position, according to Odaily. Democratic Senator Elizabeth Warren has expressed concerns over Trump's actions, suggesting that his approach is creating challenges for the central bank.

data.title: U.S. March Housing Starts Exceed Expectations
data.description: The U.S. reported an annualized total of 1.502 million housing starts in March, surpassing the expected 1.4 million units, according to Jin10. The previous figure was 1.487 million units.

data.title: U.S. March Non-Defense Capital Goods Orders Excluding Aircraft Rise 3.3%
data.description: The U.S. non-defense capital goods orders excluding aircraft increased by 3.3% in March, according to Jin10. This figure surpassed the expected 0.5% growth. The previous month's data was revised from an initial 0.70% to 1.6%.

data.title: U.S. March Building Permits Total 1.372 Million Units
data.description: The total number of building permits issued in the United States in March was 1.372 million units. According to Jin10, this figure fell short of the expected 1.39 million units and was slightly lower than the previous value of 1.386 million units.

data.title: Paris Saint-Germain's chances of winning the Champions League once surpassed Bayern Munich's, but have now risen to 32%, a 6% increase in the last 24 hours.
data.description: According to Odaily Seer, in Polymarket's "UEFA Champions League Winner" prediction event, Paris Saint-Germain's probability of winning the title briefly surpassed Bayern Munich's, but has now risen to 32%, level with Bayern Munich. This represents a 6% increase in the past 24 hours, while Bayern Munich's probability of winning has decreased by 5% in the past 24 hours. Arsenal and Atletico Madrid currently have probabilities of winning at 27% and 11% respectively. To date, the total trading volume for "UEFA Champions League Winner" contracts has exceeded $248 million. In the first leg of the Champions League semi-final, Paris Saint-Germain narrowly defeated Bayern Munich 5-4 at home, holding a slight advantage over Bayern Munich in the two-legged tie. The second leg will be played in Munich on May 6th. The first leg of the other semi-final will be played on April 29th, with Atletico Madrid hosting Arsenal. Odaily Seer continues to monitor the prediction market, seeing changes before pricing.

data.title: Visa integrates Polygon stablecoin settlement, with an annualized scale of $7 billion.
data.description: Payments giant Visa has officially integrated Polygon into its global stablecoin settlement program, allowing its card issuers and acquirers to now complete stablecoin settlements via the Polygon network. Polygon states it has become the world's largest USD stablecoin payment network, handling 34% of USD stablecoin transfers and 54% of USDC transfers; it boasts approximately 3.19 million weekly active stablecoin users and an on-chain stablecoin supply of $3.62 billion; and 178.1 million USD stablecoin transactions were completed in March. Visa's stablecoin settlement program achieved an annualized operating scale of $7 billion this quarter, a 50% increase compared to three months ago. Polygon emphasizes that its network features tiered transaction fees for USD and Asia, approximately 4-second transaction confirmation times, and is already being used by institutions such as Stripe, Revolut, Flutterwave, and BlackRock for real-world financial settlements.

data.title: Blockchain financial company Fence has raised $20 million in funding, led by Galaxy Digital.
data.description: Blockchain finance company Fence announced the completion of a new $20 million funding round, led by Galaxy Digital, with participation from Parafi Capital and Crane Venture Partners. Fence focuses on transforming the back-office processes of the asset-backed finance market using blockchain and tokenization technologies, primarily targeting the operational layer of structured lending transactions, including loan pool tracking, collateral verification, and cash flow. Fence emphasizes that it does not define itself as a blockchain company, but rather uses blockchain as a back-office infrastructure to automatically manage cash, collateral, and transaction rules through smart contracts, and tokenizes loan positions when necessary to improve liquidity and automation. (CoinDesk)

data.title: Galaxy Digital Leads $20 Million Investment in Blockchain Startup Fence
data.description: Galaxy Digital has spearheaded a $20 million funding round for Fence, a startup focused on developing blockchain software for structured credit operations. According to NS3.AI, Fence aims to modernize the $6 trillion asset-backed finance market, which currently depends heavily on manual processes involving spreadsheets, PDFs, and emails. The company plans to use the new capital to expand its operations in the U.S. and reports that its platform is already managing approximately $1.5 billion in assets.

data.title: PRECIOUS METALS | International Spot Gold Hits Monthly Low Amid Rising Oil Prices
data.description: International spot gold prices have reached a new monthly low as oil prices continue to rise, according to Jin10. Despite a brief rebound, sustained optimism in the gold market will require stability before upward momentum can be achieved.
